examples.title: Array Basic Operations
examples.description: Complete program demonstrating array declaration, initialization, traversal, and common operations
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates: 1) Array declaration with new keyword, 2) Initialization with curly braces, 3) Accessing elements using index, 4) Using length property, 5) Traditional for loop, 6) Enhanced for-each loop, 7) Arrays.toString() for printing, 8) Arrays.fill() for filling values
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for traversal, O(n) for fill

examples.title: Linear and Binary Search
examples.description: Implementation of both linear and binary search algorithms in Java
examples.explanation: Linear search checks each element sequentially until target is found. Binary search repeatedly divides sorted array in half, comparing middle element with target. Binary search is much faster for large arrays but requires sorted data.

examples.title: Bubble Sort Implementation
examples.description: Complete bubble sort algorithm with optimization for already sorted arrays
examples.explanation: Bubble sort repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if in wrong order. After each pass, largest unsorted element 'bubbles up' to correct position. Optimization: if no swaps in a pass, array is already sorted.

examples.title: Two-Dimensional Array Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates declaration, initialization, and traversal of 2D arrays including jagged arrays
examples.explanation: 2D arrays in Java are arrays of arrays. Regular 2D arrays have fixed columns per row. Jagged arrays have variable column counts per row, providing flexibility. Both use nested loops for traversal.
